name: Abe Lyman
abstract: Abe Lyman (born Abraham Simon; August 4, 1897 – October 23, 1957) was a bandleader from the 1920s to the 1940s. He made recordings, appeared in films and provided the music for numerous radio shows, including Your Hit Parade.

about_quotes.speaker: Billy Bauer
about_quotes.evidence: I did it with Abe Lyman too. ... I was with, Abe Lyman ... And in Abe Lyman’s band we got in the USO.
about_quotes.doc_title: Billy Bauer interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, March 22, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee only confirms a familial connection ('sister-in-law') between the interviewer's subjects (Abe Lyman and others), without stating any personal interaction or relationship with Abe Lyman himself.

about_quotes.speaker: Joe Thomas
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewer mentions Abe Lyman; the interviewee (Joe Thomas) does not mention Abe Lyman directly nor describe a personal interaction, only responding to the interviewer's narrative about the event.

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: Lyman wired me in New York to come and put the band in there. So when I came back to put the band in there, we put Mutt and them in there--Kid Ory and them in there.

about_quotes.speaker: Arthur "Monk" Hazel
about_quotes.evidence: Not Abe Lyman, but the other. Not, uh, not, uh, not Lyman. Uh, oh, Arnheim. Gus Arnheim.
